mettaṁ.gram: adjective/noun (accusative or neuter singular form of metta, from mitta "friend")
breakdown: Root sense: friend (mitta); metta is the quality of a true friend, goodwill.

mettā.meaning: loving kindness; benevolence; goodwill towards all beings
breakdown: Friend; mettā is the friendliness of heart extended towards all beings.
mettā.goenka: Mettā, loving kindness, is one of the four brahmavihāras — mettā, karuṇā, muditā, upekkhā — and one of the ten pāramīs that a meditator develops. Klaus recalls how the Buddha gently touched the charging elephant Nālāgiri with an all-suffusing mettā. The practice of mettā-bhāvanā is the conscious development of this quality directed in all directions: mettāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, "one dwells pervading one direction with a mind filled with mettā". In a Vipassana course mettā-bhāvanā is practised at the close of each sitting, sharing with all beings the peace and harmony generated by the purification of the mind.

mettāsahagatena.meaning: with (a mind) filled with loving kindness; accompanied by mettā
mettāsahagatena.summary: From the brahmavihāra formula: mettāsahagatena cetasā, "with a mind suffused with mettā".
breakdown: Gone (past participle); sahagata is literally "going together with", filled with.
Wrong, false, contrary to truth; the opposite of sammā (right).

micchattavaggavaṇṇanā, mahāvagga-aṭṭhaka.meaning: the commentary on the Micchatta chapter, in the Mahāvagga commentary of the Saṁyuttanikāya
breakdown: The commentary on the Great Division.

micchādiṭṭhiggahaṇaṁ.meaning: grasping at wrong view; the holding of false views
breakdown: Holding, grasping, seizing. (The doubling gg is junction doubling.)

micchāpaṭipadañca.meaning: and the wrong way of practice; and wrongly walking the path
micchāpaṭipadañca.gram: feminine noun, compound (accusative singular) + enclitic ca
breakdown: Way of practice, manner of walking the path.
And. (The niggahīta ṁ + ca becomes ñca by sandhi.)

micchāpaṭipadā.meaning: the wrong path; wrong way of practice
breakdown: Manner of walking, path, way of practice.

micchāpaṭipattādhikaraṇahetu.meaning: in consequence of and by reason of wrong practice
breakdown: In consequence of, on account of.

micchāsaṅkappagocarā.meaning: dwelling in the sphere of wrong thoughts

midhekacco.meaning: here a certain one; in this case someone
midhekacco.gram: sandhi fusion: junction consonant m + adverb + adjective (m + idha + ekacco)
breakdown: Junction consonant (sandhi insertion after a preceding word).

milakkhesu.meaning: among barbarians; among outcast border peoples

missībhāvo.meaning: union; a mixed state, becoming mingled

mittadubbho.meaning: betrayer of friends; one who deceives or hurts a friend

mittapatirūpakā.meaning: false friends; those who merely have the appearance of being a friend
mittapatirūpakā.summary: From the Siṅgālasutta's teaching on the four kinds of false friends and four kinds of true friends.
breakdown: Resembling, counterfeit, having the mere appearance of.

modanaṭṭhāneyeva.meaning: just in the place of rejoicing
modanaṭṭhāneyeva.gram: neuter compound (locative singular) + emphatic particle yeva
breakdown: Place. (The doubling ṭṭh is junction doubling.)

moghamañña.gram: compound: adjective + noun (mogha + añña, with junction consonant m)

moghapurisā.summary: A term of rebuke in the suttas: "foolish man!"

mohajañcāpaviddasu.meaning: and what is born of delusion... the ignorant person
mohajañcāpaviddasu.gram: sandhi chain: compound adjective + particles + noun (mohajaṁ + cāpi + aviddasu)

breakdown: Destruction, extinction. (The doubling kkh is junction doubling.)

mohavinayapariyosāno.meaning: ending in the driving away of delusion; culminating in the removal of illusion

breakdown: Root: to be confused, deluded (muyhati); strengthened to moh-.
moho.goenka: Moha, delusion or ignorance, is one of the three roots of the unwholesome together with lobha (greed) and dosa (aversion). It is the not-knowing of reality as it is — taking the impermanent to be lasting, the suffering to be pleasant, the non-self to be self. Vipassanā works directly against moha: by observing the arising and passing away of sensations, the meditator replaces delusion with paññā, direct experiential wisdom.

muddhābhisittassa.meaning: of the crowned king; of one anointed on the head
breakdown: Anointed, consecrated (past participle of abhisiñcati); the anointed king.

muhuttaṁ.meaning: a moment; a minute, a short while
muhuttaṁ.gram: masculine/neuter noun (accusative singular of muhutta, used adverbially: "for a moment")

breakdown: Quotation marker (iti), closing the quoted expression.
Mouth, face; figuratively the entrance or leading cause of something.

mukhapāṭhena.meaning: by oral recitation; by recitation from the mouth

mukhasattīhi.meaning: with the weapons of the mouth; with verbal daggers

musāvādo.goenka: Abstaining from musāvāda, false speech, is one of the five precepts of sīla and part of sammā-vācā, right speech, in the Noble Eightfold Path. Truthfulness is the foundation: one whose speech is false cannot develop the inner honesty that vipassanā demands, since the practice itself is the observation of truth — the reality of mind and body as it actually is, from moment to moment.

musāvādā.meaning: from false speech (as in the precept of abstaining from lying)
musāvādā.gram: masculine noun, compound (ablative singular: "from false speech")

muttomhi.meaning: I am liberated; I am released
muttomhi.gram: sandhi fusion: past participle + verb (mutto + amhi "I am")
breakdown: I am. (The final -o fuses with a-: mutto + amhi → muttomhi.)

muttosi.gram: sandhi fusion: past participle + verb (mutto + asi "you are")
breakdown: You are. (The final -o fuses with a-: mutto + asi → muttosi.)

muṇḍe.gram: adjective (locative singular or plural form of muṇḍa)

māgaviko.gram: masculine agent noun (from maga "deer, wild animal"; nominative singular)
breakdown: Agent suffix: one who hunts deer.

mākattha.meaning: do not perform!; you should not do
mākattha.gram: prohibitive: particle mā + aorist 2nd person plural of karoti
breakdown: You did/should do (aorist 2nd person plural of karoti); with mā it expresses prohibition.

mālāgandhavilepanassa.meaning: of garlands, scents and perfumes (embellishments and decorations)

mālāguḷaparikkhittāpi.meaning: even though encircled with clusters of garlands

māneti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular present, causative of maññati (√man)
breakdown: Root: to think; the causative māneti is "to hold in esteem", to honour.
